title: ROS2 Fundamentals: Program Real Robots
subtitle: The language of professional robotics. Learn it properly.
description: ROS2 is what runs robots in Amazon warehouses, Boston Dynamics labs, and space missions. This course teaches you the full stack — nodes, topics, services, navigation, perception, and hardware integration — using a browser-based Gazebo simulator so you need no physical hardware to complete the course.
what_youll_learn: Understand ROS2 architecture: nodes, topics, services, and actions
Write publisher and subscriber nodes in Python
Describe a robot with URDF and visualise it in RViz2
Use the Nav2 stack for autonomous navigation with A* path planning
Process camera data with OpenCV inside a ROS2 node
Fuse lidar and camera data for robust obstacle detection
Run a full Gazebo simulation of a warehouse navigation robot
Debug ROS2 systems with rqt, ros2 topic echo, and TF tree inspection
waitlist_cta: Join the Forge waitlist — first 100 get early access
